front: Short Story: the Grandmother tells an escaped convict 'you're one of my babies, you're one of my own children' before he shoots her three times in the chest

front: Short Story: Julian's racist mother wears the same purple hat as a Black woman on the bus, then dies of a stroke after offering the woman's child a penny
back: Everything That Rises Must Converge (Flannery O'Connor)

front: Short Story: the protagonist's mother mentions her grandfather owned 200 slaves on the bus to the YMCA for reducing classes
back: Everything That Rises Must Converge (Flannery O'Connor)

front: Short Story: a Bible salesman named Manley Pointer steals a woman's wooden leg after seducing her in a barn loft; his hollowed-out Bible contains whiskey
Short Story: a philosophy PhD changed her name from Joy to spite her mother; she tells her mother 'Malebranche was right: we are not our own light'
Novel: the veteran protagonist founds the 'Church Without Christ' in Taulkinham
Novel: Enoch Emery steals a shriveled mummy from a museum to serve as the 'new Jesus,' then murders an actor playing Gonga the Gorilla and steals his suit
Novel: Asa Hawks fakes being blind (abandoned his plan to blind himself with quicklime); his daughter Sabbath Lily cradles the stolen mummy
Novel: a teenager simultaneously drowns and baptizes the mentally disabled boy Bishop during a fishing trip
back: The Violent Bear It Away (Flannery O'Connor)

front: Novel: the great-uncle Mason kidnaps the protagonist (named after the Swamp Fox) to raise him as a prophet
back: The Violent Bear It Away (Flannery O'Connor)

front: Short Story: Mary Grace throws a book at Ruby Turpin in a doctor's waiting room and calls her a 'wart hog from hell,' triggering a vision of a heavenly procession
Short Story: one-armed Tom Shiftlet marries deaf Lucynell Crater and abandons her at a roadside restaurant during their honeymoon
back: The Life You Save May Be Your Own (Flannery O'Connor)
work: The Life You Save May Be Your Own

front: Short Story: Mrs. May shouts 'Jesus stab me in the heart' and is then literally gored to death by a bull
Short Story: the hoodlum Rufus convinces Norton he can see his dead mother through a telescope, leading Norton to hang himself
back: The Lame Shall Enter First (Flannery O'Connor)

front: Short Story: O. E. Parker (Obadiah Elihue) gets a Byzantine tattoo of Christ on his back; his wife Sarah Ruth beats him with a broom for idolatry
Author: lived with over 40 peacocks on the estate Andalusia in Milledgeville, Georgia; wrote the essay 'The King of the Birds'
Author: reworked the Iowa Writers' Workshop thesis story 'The Geranium' into 'Judgment Day'
